title: The endpoint case of the Bennett-Carbery-Tao multilinear Kakeya conjecture

thm:1.summary: Endpoint multilinear Kakeya (geometric form): if $T_{j,a}\subset\mathbb{R}^n$ ($1\le j\le n$) are unit cylinders with the core of $T_{j,a}$ making angle $\le(100n)^{-1}$ with the $x_j$-axis, then the set $I$ of points lying in at least one cylinder from each of the $n$ directions has measure $|I|\lesssim\prod_j A(j)^{1/(n-1)}$ — with no $\epsilon$ loss, closing the endpoint left open by Bennett–Carbery–Tao.

thm:2.summary: Endpoint multilinear Kakeya (estimate form): for $n$ families of unit cylinders with cores $v_{j,a}$ that are $\theta$-transverse ($|\det(v_{1,a_1},\dots,v_{n,a_n})|\ge\theta$), $\|\prod_{j=1}^n(\sum_a\chi_{T_{j,a}})\|_{L^{n/(n-1)}(\mathbb{R}^n)}\lesssim\theta^{-1/(n-1)}\prod_j A(j)^{1/(n-1)}$. The sharp $L^{n/(n-1)}$ inequality.

sec:1.summary: The polynomial ham sandwich theorem (Stone–Tukey 1941, popularized by Gromov): any $\binom{n+d}{d}-1$ measurable sets in $\mathbb{R}^n$ can be simultaneously bisected by an algebraic hypersurface of degree $\le d$. The single external tool the proof rests on — used to produce a low-degree variety passing through the configuration of cylinder intersections.

lem:2.1.summary: Cylinder estimate: if $T$ is a radius-$r$ cylinder with core direction $v$ and $Z$ is a degree-$d$ algebraic hypersurface, the directed volume satisfies $V_{Z\cap T}(v)\le\omega_{n-1}r^{n-1}d$. Bounds how much a low-degree variety can 'see' along a tube's axis.

lem:2.2.summary: Near-axis volume bound: if $v_1,\dots,v_n$ are unit vectors with $|e_j-v_j|<(100n)^{-1}$, then for any hypersurface $S$, $\mathrm{Vol}(S)\le2\sum_{j=1}^n V_S(v_j)$. Lets ordinary volume be controlled by the $n$ axis-directed volumes.

lem:5.1.summary: Properties of the mollified directed volume $\bar V_{Z\cap U}(v)$ and mollified visibility $\overline{\mathrm{Vis}}[Z\cap U]$: homogeneity ($\bar V(\lambda v)=|\lambda|\bar V(v)$), convexity in $v$, and additivity over disjoint unions of $U$. The functional-analytic regularity needed before pigeonholing.

lem:5.2.summary: Visibility lemma: for a suitable region $E'$ there is some coordinate $j$ with $\bar V_{Z\cap E'}(e_j)\gtrsim\mathrm{Vol}(E')/E_j'$ — a single direction in which the variety is highly visible. The dichotomy that drives the induction.

lem:6.1.summary: Per-cube multilinear bound: for each cube $Q_k$ in the decomposition, $\overline{\mathrm{Vis}}[Z\cap Q_k]<C(n)\theta^{-1}\prod_{j=1}^n\bar V_{Z\cap Q_k}(v_{j,k})$. The local multilinear Kakeya inequality for the variety; summing over cubes gives thm:1/thm:2.

lem:7.1.summary: Box estimate for unions of tubes: for any algebraic hypersurface $Z'$ of degree $\lesssim L$, the average of $V_{Z'\cap B(x,1)}(v)$ over $x\in T$ is $\lesssim1$. The degree-vs-thickness trade controlling the contribution of high-degree pieces.

edges.note: The $L^{n/(n-1)}$ estimate is the dual/level-set repackaging of the point-set measure bound.
A degree-controlled algebraic hypersurface $Z$ adapted to the cylinder configuration is produced by the polynomial ham sandwich theorem.
Summing the per-cube multilinear visibility bound over a decomposition yields the global bound on $|I|$.
Ordinary volume of $Z$ inside each cube is converted to the $n$ axis-directed volumes.
The box estimate handles the contribution of the (high-degree) algebraic hypersurface across tubes.
The per-cube bound is built from the visibility-dichotomy lemma applied within each cube.
Each directed volume $\bar V_{Z\cap Q_k}(v_{j,k})$ is controlled by the cylinder estimate (degree $\times$ radius).
The visibility dichotomy uses homogeneity, convexity and additivity of the mollified directed volume.
Volume is split into axis-directed pieces to find the highly-visible direction.
The box estimate is an averaged form of the cylinder estimate.

main_results.description: The endpoint multilinear Kakeya inequality, in point-set and $L^{n/(n-1)}$ forms.

polynomial_method.description: The polynomial ham sandwich theorem — the algebraic tool that produces a low-degree variety adapted to the cylinder configuration.

directed_volume_and_visibility.description: Directed volume of a variety along a direction, the cylinder/degree estimate, the near-axis volume bound, the regularity of mollified directed volume, and the visibility dichotomy.

degree_reduction.description: The box estimate trading polynomial degree against tube thickness.

BCT.title: On the multilinear restriction and Kakeya conjectures

Dvir:FiniteField.title: On the size of Kakeya sets in finite fields

Gromov:SingularitiesExpanders.title: Singularities, expanders and topology of maps
